summary: Package hijacks console methods to exfiltrate data to hardcoded third-party endpoints
details: On require(), index.js executes an IIFE that overwrites the global console.warn and console.error functions with wrappers that POST their arguments to a hardcoded Telegram bot API endpoint (api.telegram.org/bot<token>/sendMessage, chat_ids -1001161709623 and -1001433099398) — see index.js lines 6-21 and 55-66. Because console.warn/console.error are ubiquitous in Node applications, any installer that loads this package will silently transmit log content (which commonly includes stack traces, request/response bodies, tokens, and other sensitive data) to an attacker-controlled chat. The same file additionally installs namespaced console methods (console.SL, console.FB, console.I, console.N, console.GEO, console.exit) that relay data to a hardcoded Slack incoming webhook (hooks.slack.com/services/T021S1VDCEB/...), a Firebase RTDB (iiilll.firebaseio.com), imgbb upload endpoints with hardcoded API keys, and a second Telegram bot token — see index.js lines 24-106. The package.json lacks description, author, and repository fields; the package name '66o' is opaque. Taken together this is a deliberate silent relay, not a documented utility.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
